content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

March 8, 1991

Dave Peri, Treasurer  
Hart for Senate  
P.O. Box 22557  
Santa Barbara, CA 93121

Re: Our Files No. I-90-164, I-90-192

Dear Mr. Peri:

Last year Ms. Elaine Miller of the Hart for Senate campaign sought written advice regarding the campaign’s duties and responsibilities under provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") concerning campaign contributions and campaign disclosure statements. The questions concerned whether a fund-raiser for the senator could be held in a particular fashion and the proper method for reporting of contributions received at the fund-raiser. Advice had been previously provided by telephone.

It is our understanding that the event took place before any written advice was provided. Because the Commission advice is prospective in its application and does not address matters concerning past conduct (Regulation 18329, copy enclosed), no further written response was provided.

After contacting Ms. Miller by telephone, we were informed that the matter had been resolved to the campaign’s satisfaction and that no further advice from the Commission was necessary. Therefore, we consider the advice requests to be withdrawn.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken
Enclosures
